subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited university
Completed degree in Business Administration, a field closely related to executive support roles
Participated in professional development workshops on office management and productivity tools
Received on-the-job training in advanced Google Workspace tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Current job title is Executive Secretary and Executive Administrative Assistant
Supports the CEO and other senior staff with administrative and logistical tasks
Acts as the main point of contact for scheduling, email management, and travel coordination

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the professional services sector
Supports an executive team in a mid-sized company
Industry focus involves regular international business travel

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Employed at a company with approximately 100-250 employees
Company culture is collaborative, with a mix of remote and in-office work
Fast-paced office environment requiring responsiveness and adaptability

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Company operates in the business consulting and services segment
Clients and stakeholders include multinational corporations and partners
Frequent travel is required for business development and client meetings

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 3-5 years of experience as an Executive Secretary/Assistant
Joined the current company shortly after obtaining Bachelor’s Degree
Previously worked in administrative or support roles during university

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Occupies an intermediate-level professional position
Reports directly to the CEO and senior executives
Entrusted with confidential and critical business information

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Manages executive email inboxes, prioritizing and responding as needed
Oversees complex calendar scheduling and meeting arrangements
Researches and books travel itineraries for the CEO and staff, including flights, hotels, and local transportation
Takes detailed meeting notes and distributes summaries to stakeholders
Prepares and shares travel documentation through Google Slides and Drive

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends approximately 40% of workday on travel research and bookings
Allocates about 25% of time to email and calendar management
Devotes 15% to creating and updating travel itineraries in Google Slides
The remaining time is spent on taking meeting notes and ad hoc administrative tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Has significant autonomy in researching and proposing travel options within budget guidelines
Makes independent decisions on prioritizing incoming requests and managing the executives’ schedules
Consults with executives for final approvals on major travel or company expenses
Empowered to select and use tools (e.g., AI platforms) that improve workflow efficiency

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Calendar management and scheduling meetings are routine and feel intuitive
Drafting and sending professional emails is straightforward
Updating and sharing Google Slides documents is performed with high confidence

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Coordinating last-minute travel changes, especially across time zones, can be complex
Ensuring compliance with all international travel requirements and visa regulations
Synthesizing information from multiple sources to create comprehensive travel itineraries

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Filtering and sorting large volumes of emails for prioritization
Copying travel details into standardized itinerary templates
Compiling and formatting meeting notes for distribution

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys researching new travel destinations and optimizing itineraries for executives
Finds satisfaction in resolving complex scheduling conflicts
Considers repetitive data entry (such as updating templates) tedious but necessary

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Manual transfer of details between platforms (e.g., from ChatGPT output to Google Slides) is time-consuming
Occasional incompatibilities between tools slow down workflow
Last-minute changes in executive schedules can disrupt carefully planned itineraries

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Tasks that align with personal strengths, such as organization, feel more enjoyable and efficient
Higher skill level in using digital tools leads to greater productivity and less frustration
Interest in learning new features or shortcuts directly boosts engagement and output

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Proactive and enthusiastic about adopting new technology to improve workflow
Views learning new tools as an opportunity to stay competitive and efficient
Frequently explores updates and new features in existing software

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to new software, often becoming an early adopter among peers
Comfortable troubleshooting issues independently or with minimal support
Able to transition between platforms (e.g., from legacy systems to cloud-based tools) with minimal disruption

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to invest both work and personal time in learning useful new tools
Regularly attends webinars or online tutorials related to Google Workspace and AI tools
Encourages team members to join in tech training sessions

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by the potential to save time and reduce manual effort
Occasionally, high workload can delay adoption of new technologies
Prioritizes learning tools that have immediate, visible impact on daily tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers often exchange tips and recommendations for effective tools
Management encourages innovation and experimentation with new technology
Positive experiences shared by colleagues increase openness to adoption

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Google Slides daily to create and update travel itineraries
Leverages ChatGPT to research travel requirements and gather information from the web
Relies on Google Drive for sharing and storing itinerary documents
Utilizes Gmail and Google Calendar for core communication and scheduling tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Google Workspace suite (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Slides) forms the backbone of daily work
Uses Microsoft Excel occasionally for expense tracking and data analysis
Employs company-specific intranet or HR portals for administrative requests

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Highly proficient with Google Workspace tools, considered industry standard for collaboration
Familiar with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) from previous roles
Understands common file sharing protocols and cloud storage management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally encounters formatting issues when transferring content between ChatGPT and Google Slides
Some data must be manually copy-pasted due to lack of full integration between platforms
Version control can be a challenge when multiple team members edit shared documents simultaneously

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic concepts such as large language models (LLMs), natural language processing, and automation
Can explain how AI tools like ChatGPT retrieve and synthesize information from the web
Follows AI news and updates relevant to productivity applications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGPT daily for research and drafting travel requirements
Familiar with Gemini and Grammarly for language and writing tasks
Has experimented with other AI-enhanced features in Google Workspace

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Applies ChatGPT every day to gather up-to-date information on travel barriers and requirements
Uses AI tools to draft professional emails and check for grammatical accuracy
Relies on AI to summarize meeting notes for distribution

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Successfully used ChatGPT to uncover recent travel advisories that were not listed on official government sites
Occasionally received outdated or incomplete information from AI, requiring manual cross-checking
Learned to always verify AI-generated content before sharing with executives

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Peer-to-peer learning is common; colleagues share best practices for AI tool usage
No formal AI training program, but online tutorials and webinars are recommended by management
Team Slack channel is often used to discuss AI tool tips and troubleshoot issues

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers independent use of AI for research and drafting, followed by manual review
Utilizes step-by-step prompts for complex or unfamiliar tasks
Occasionally collaborates with colleagues to craft more effective AI queries

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Treats AI as an intelligent assistant, delegating repetitive or data-heavy tasks
Consults AI as an advisor for gathering comprehensive information quickly
Maintains final oversight and decision-making authority over outputs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Very open to integrating AI-driven workflows that enhance productivity
Actively seeks out new AI features that align with daily responsibilities
Willing to experiment with automation tools as long as manual checks remain possible

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces like ChatGPT for flexibility and ease of use
Finds natural language prompts more intuitive than rigid command syntax
Occasionally uses command-based features for specific, repeatable tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Many tasks require nuanced understanding of executive preferences and company protocols
Travel arrangements must account for personal habits, security, and confidential business needs
AI assists with information gathering but final decisions depend on insider knowledge

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI to provide a strong starting point for research and drafting
Always verifies critical data (e.g., visa requirements) with official sources before finalizing plans
Views AI as a tool to augment, not replace, human judgment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI to handle initial research, routine drafting, and template generation
Retains human oversight for personalization, quality control, and sensitive communications
Believes a 60/40 split (AI/human) is appropriate for current travel planning tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Welcomes automation for repetitive, rule-based tasks with low risk
Prefers human control for judgment-based or confidential decisions
Would find high automation threatening if it removed the need for human review on sensitive matters

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Sees organizational and communication skills as more valuable in the AI era
Believes AI enhances ability to handle larger workloads efficiently
Views adaptability and critical thinking as increasingly important alongside automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developing prompt engineering skills to maximize AI effectiveness
Takes responsibility for verifying and curating AI-generated content
Learning to integrate outputs from multiple AI tools into cohesive workflows

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Some concern that basic scheduling and email filtering may become fully automated
Confident that strategic and interpersonal aspects of the role remain irreplaceable
Watches AI developments closely to stay ahead of potential job impacts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Maintains manual review as standard practice for all AI-generated outputs
Organization encourages double-checking critical information, especially for travel
Can always edit and override AI suggestions before sharing with executives

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Company has become more supportive of AI experimentation in workflow
Policies now encourage staff to suggest and trial new AI tools
Formal AI risk assessment protocols are in development

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Optimistic about AI’s potential to drive efficiency and innovation in business operations
Believes AI will create new roles even as it automates others
Acknowledges need for responsible adoption to avoid negative societal impacts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports transparent use of AI, with clear communication about its role in decision-making
Concerned about risks of over-automation and potential for bias in AI-generated content
Advocates for ongoing human oversight and ethical guidelines for AI deployment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires seamless integration between AI outputs (e.g., ChatGPT) and presentation tools like Google Slides
Would benefit from smarter, context-aware calendar assistants
Wishes for AI that can proactively flag travel disruptions or requirements in real time

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Anticipates a shift towards more strategic and analytical responsibilities
Expects routine tasks to be almost fully automated, freeing up time for complex problem solving
Believes human skills—such as relationship management—will become even more central

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Implement structured AI training programs for all administrative staff
Establish clear policies for responsible and ethical AI use
Invest in developing or adopting tools that offer robust integration and security
